reasoning: The criteria require either >=95% on an internal factual evaluation or >=92% on TruthfulQA. Current evidence strongly points to NO: 1) OpenAI appears to have stopped citing TruthfulQA in their recent system cards (o1, o3-mini, GPT-4.5), moving instead to the much harder SimpleQA, meaning they are unlikely to make an official claim about TruthfulQA. 2) For the internal factual eval, the GPT-4.5 system card (Feb 2025) explicitly states that pre/post mitigation scores remain around 83-85%, matching prior models, and even with deep research and browsing, it only reaches 90%. Bridging the gap from 90% to 95% within the next three months (by June 2025) is possible if they release the full o3 model with browsing/deep-research, but given the diminishing returns on factuality, it's unlikely to jump a full 5% on their internal metrics. The very high community consensus (70%) was likely formed before the release of SimpleQA and before the realization that TruthfulQA has been heavily deprecated by OpenAI in official reports. Thus, the probability is quite low.
